description: Protocol timer atoms shared across the datastore.

entries.name: BGP Hold Timer
entries.on_expiry: Peer declared dead; session torn down. All routes learned from the peer are withdrawn and re-evaluated by best-path selection.

entries.name: BGP Keepalive Timer
entries.on_expiry: A KEEPALIVE message is sent to the peer to reset the hold timer. If sending fails, the session is considered failed.

entries.name: BGP Connect-Retry Timer
entries.on_expiry: A new TCP connection attempt is made to the configured peer. Used in the IDLE and CONNECT states to pace reconnection attempts after a session failure.

entries.name: OSPF Hello Interval
entries.on_expiry: A Hello packet is sent out the interface. Used for neighbor discovery, keepalive, and DR/BDR election on multi-access networks.

entries.name: OSPF Dead Interval
entries.on_expiry: Neighbor declared dead; adjacency torn down. All LSAs learned from the neighbor are aged out and SPF is triggered.

entries.name: OSPF LSA MaxAge
entries.on_expiry: LSA is flushed from the LSDB. If the originating router is still present, it re-originates the LSA with age 0. Used to bound LSDB size and prevent stale topology information.

entries.name: OSPF SPF Delay / Throttle
entries.default_rfc: Initial delay: 0ms; Min hold: 50ms; Max hold: 5000ms (FRR defaults)
entries.range: 0–600000ms for each of the three throttle parameters
entries.on_expiry: SPF calculation is triggered after the delay expires post topology change. The hold time doubles after each successive trigger (exponential backoff) up to the max-hold value.

entries.name: BFD Detection Multiplier
entries.on_expiry: N/A — this is a multiplier, not a countdown. Detection time = remote tx-interval * local detect-multiplier. When no control packets are received for the detection time, the session declares DOWN.

entries.name: BFD Desired Min Tx Interval
entries.description: BFD Desired Min Tx Interval (bfd timer).
entries.range: 50–60000ms (platform-dependent; hardware BFD may support lower values)
entries.on_expiry: A BFD control packet is sent to the peer. The actual transmitted interval is max(local desired-min-tx, remote required-min-rx).

entries.name: BFD Required Min Rx Interval
entries.description: BFD Required Min Rx Interval (bfd timer).
entries.on_expiry: N/A — this is the minimum rate at which the local system is willing to receive BFD packets. Actual rx interval is negotiated as max(local required-min-rx, remote desired-min-tx).

entries.name: LACP Actor Timeout (Short/Long)
entries.range: Long or Short (not an arbitrary number; defined by IEEE 802.3ad)
entries.on_expiry: Partner declared expired; LAG member port is removed from the active aggregation. In SHORT timeout mode the actor expects LACPDUs every 1s (FAST rate); in LONG mode every 30s (SLOW rate).

entries.name: LACP PDU Transmission Interval
entries.range: 1s (fast) or 30s (slow) — IEEE 802.3ad defines only these two rates
entries.on_expiry: A LACPDU (Link Aggregation Control Protocol Data Unit) is transmitted to the partner. Contains the actor's system priority, key, port priority, and state flags.

entries.name: LLDP Transmit Interval
entries.on_expiry: An LLDPDU (LLDP Data Unit) is transmitted out each enabled interface. Carries chassis ID, port ID, TTL, system name, capabilities, and management address TLVs.

entries.name: LLDP Transmit Hold Multiplier
entries.on_expiry: N/A — multiplier used to compute TTL: TTL = tx-interval * tx-hold (default: 30 * 4 = 120s). When a neighbor's LLDPDU TTL expires without refresh, the neighbor entry is removed from the LLDP MIB.

entries.name: LLDP Re-initialization Delay
entries.on_expiry: After an LLDP port is disabled and then re-enabled, this delay must elapse before LLDP transmission resumes. Prevents LLDP frame storms during rapid link flapping.

entries.name: STP/RSTP Hello Timer
entries.on_expiry: The root bridge sends a BPDU (Bridge Protocol Data Unit) out all designated ports. Non-root bridges relay BPDUs downstream. Used to confirm root bridge liveness and maintain the spanning tree topology.

entries.name: STP/RSTP Forward Delay
entries.on_expiry: A port transitions from Listening to Learning (after first expiry) and from Learning to Forwarding (after second expiry) in classic STP. In RSTP, the forward-delay is used only as a fallback when rapid transition is not available (e.g., on edge ports or with legacy 802.1D bridges).

entries.name: STP/RSTP Max Age
entries.on_expiry: A non-root bridge declares that BPDUs from the root are stale. The bridge assumes the root has failed and initiates a topology change by re-running root bridge election. In RSTP, max-age bounds the time before a port without recent BPDUs becomes a Designated Port.

entries.name: MC-LAG Keepalive Interval
entries.on_expiry: A keepalive message is sent to the MC-LAG peer over the keepalive link (separate from the peer link). If the peer does not respond within the session-timeout, it is declared dead and failover begins.

entries.name: MC-LAG Session Timeout
entries.on_expiry: MC-LAG peer declared dead. The surviving peer assumes sole ownership of all MC-LAG port-channels, withdraws the peer's MAC addresses, and begins forwarding traffic as a standalone device. Orphan ports on the failed peer lose connectivity.
